evidence.text:
226
-3-

book was released in 74. Put this way, it is easy to see what the dream in the book meant. Also, a person versed in apocalyptic writing would, in reading the dream, know what it meant (by and large). Therefore it is possible that the message (the dream) did reach some kind of audience, which then knew what to expect, or what was already at that time taking place; prediction or analysis, depending on when they read it. It was virtually synchronized, which strikes me as very very meaningf

11
11

In Tears it was Jason Taverner - + he was (after the king + knights dream) found innocent. + the tyrant, represented by Buckman, fell. The whole system fell. Now, with [better] clarity, I can see why, in Tears, the dream had to be there: it marked the intervention by God, signalling the fall of the prison; i.e. the breaching through, the intervention, the change - exactly as was to occur here in our world 3 or 4 years later. Tears is a depictment of all which was very soon actually to occ

Indexed
10
10

Then the Xerox missive is to be understood as the flushing into view of B, its quarry. Buckman, in "Tears," is a portrait, synthetic or real, of that quarry, that "intelligence officer in the army," pitted against me, "Jason Taverner"! "Firebright" is the C seed placed in me, a micro version of telos inserted into atelos to start harmonia + kosmos growing. Like logos spermatika. C is a two-stage life form: seed + plant. __ To boil it down, C (which is rational) hunts its victim B

10

So "Tears" the period of Acts and out world underwent corresponding transformations which are roughly similar, especially if christians are equals with the anti-war country culture (which is mentioned in "tears"- the students and forced labor camps, etc). The critical moment comes; the critical moment goes- things do not stand at the end of Acts as they start at the beginning. An orderly revolution, under the jurisdiction of the spirit (anticipated in John), has taken place: the inner citade

exculpatory bottom line message smuggled through the occlusions in "Tears". No wonder that in 3-74 I saw the word "Felix" as the key word. It is, Happy. + Jason Taverner (i.e. Dionysos - a drinker) being found innocent. So I wasn't emotionally programmed to see it. But that is the real message:

Not: {serious King} Felix

but: serious {King Felix}

The solution lies in the satanic inerpolation + my "backing" theory.

"The Bacchae" shows a connection between prisons + tears between joy + prison - bursting.

So our world is invisibly being transubstantiated, as the BIP + its power is ended. I experienced this in the breaking of the "Astral determinism" over, the nemesis which was beginning to arrive in 3-74, following the correct schema in "Scanner." It is precisely that schema ("they played, + were punished far too much") which Christ is abolishing (we know this as "salvation" - saving us from hell - not a bad description!)
